## What You Will Learn

Cross-validation gives a more robust estimate of model performance than a single train-test split. It reduces variance in your performance estimate and ensures your model generalizes well.

### Key Concepts
- **K-Fold Cross-Validation**: Splits data into K equal parts (folds). Trains on K-1 folds, validates on the remaining fold. Repeats K times so every fold is used for validation once.
- **Why Not Just One Split?**: A single random split might be lucky or unlucky. CV averages out this randomness.
- **shuffle=True**: Randomizes data before splitting to prevent ordering bias.
- **Mean Accuracy**: The final CV score is the average accuracy across all folds.

### Why This Matters
K-Fold CV is the standard for comparing models and estimating real-world performance. Papers, Kaggle competitions, and production pipelines all use it.
